vitals.aid_stations: 5+ (Santa Rita Road, Kiler Canyon, Halter Ranch Vineyards, Cypress Mountain Drive, Cayucos)

course_description.overview: Eroica California is a gravel cycling event on California's Central Coast featuring ocean views, wine country, and challenging mountain passes. The long route (97 miles) includes approximately 30 miles of strade bianche (gravel roads) with three timed sections, starting in Paso Robles and finishing in Cambria with coastal sunset views.

key_segments.name: Warm-up to Cayucos
key_segments.description: Flat coastal ride south to the seaside village of Cayucos with beach stop and refreshments from the Sea Shanty

key_segments.name: Santa Rita Creek Road
key_segments.description: Steep strade bianche (gravel) ascent with technical descent, first timed section

key_segments.name: Kiler Canyon
key_segments.description: Infamous steep, narrow gravel road section known for forcing experienced riders to walk; second timed section with water and snacks at intersection

key_segments.name: Halter Ranch Vineyards
key_segments.description: Rest stop at sustainable winery in Paso Robles wine country with food service during event

key_segments.name: Cypress Mountain Drive
key_segments.description: Final steep, twisty gravel climb reaching 2,200+ feet elevation with 2,000-foot white-knuckle descent back to Cambria; third timed section

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The route combines challenging gravel climbing with technical descents across California's Central Coast. Riders face sustained elevation gain (7,500 feet total), notorious sections like Kiler Canyon that test both fitness and bike handling, and the steep Cypress Mountain Drive finale. The event emphasizes 'the beauty of fatigue' with a focus on endurance and mental fortitude rather than pure speed.

logistics.getting_there: Located on California's Central Coast near Cambria and Paso Robles, accessible via Highway 101. Approximately 3-4 hours from San Francisco, 5-6 hours from Los Angeles
logistics.accommodation: Cambria and Paso Robles offer hotels, vacation rentals, and campgrounds. Event is part of a weekend festival format
logistics.registration_details: Event traditionally held as a weekend festival (Saturday-Sunday format). 2026 date listed as September with exact date TBD. Registration details not provided in available sources
logistics.bike_logistics: Vintage road bikes and modern gravel bikes both accommodated. Event includes Concours d'Elegance for vintage bicycles. Gravel-capable bikes with drop bars recommended for technical sections
logistics.local_considerations: Event features stops at local businesses including Halter Ranch Vineyards, Olea Farm, Cayucos State Beach, and local restaurants. Coastal weather typical for Central Coast in September (mild, potential fog). Event emphasizes sustainable practices and local community engagement

final_verdict.one_liner: A challenging Central Coast gravel adventure with stunning ocean and wine country views, though event continuity and logistics require verification
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced gravel cyclists seeking technical climbing, scenic coastal riding, and the Eroica philosophy of 'beauty in fatigue.' Riders comfortable with sustained gravel sections and steep descents. Vintage bike enthusiasts and cyclists seeking a lifestyle event beyond pure competition
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Potentially yes for the unique combination of technical gravel, coastal scenery, and wine country culture. However, event status is uncertain as of 2026—verify registration opening and exact dates before committing. The 2,000-person limit and weekend festival format add value, but logistics and accommodation costs may be significant
pro_tips: Verify event is running in 2026 before booking accommodation—search results indicate uncertainty about continuation
Arrive early for the weekend festival experience; the Concours d'Elegance and community atmosphere are integral to the Eroica experience
Practice technical descents before attempting Cypress Mountain Drive and Kiler Canyon; walking sections is common and acceptable
Book accommodation in Cambria or Paso Robles well in advance; Central Coast lodging fills quickly during event weekends
